title: Reproducibility tooling: independent re-run re-derives sampled claims from recorded sources/queries
context: Content-addressed provenance is only meaningful if claims are actually re-derivable. This safety-critical M4 task builds reproducibility tooling so an independent re-run, with no access to prior intermediate state, can re-derive >= 95% of a random sample of published claims from the recorded sources/queries, with the procedure documented for external parties.
objective: Implement reproducibility tooling so an independent re-run (no access to prior intermediate state) re-derives >= 95% of a random sample of published claims from the recorded sources/queries, investigates and logs misses, and documents the re-derivation procedure for external reproduction.
acceptanceCriteria: An independent re-run (no access to prior intermediate state) re-derives >= 95% of a random sample of published claims from the recorded sources/queries; misses are investigated/logged
The re-derivation procedure is documented so external parties can reproduce it
Reviewed and signed off by the provenance auditor + maintainer (recorded)
